mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 5-day forward returns following a 0.2pp UNRATE jump are statistically indistinguishable from zero or positive across 50+ historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding confirms broad-based private sector demand expansion, which lifts equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and reduced recession probability.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero directional accuracy in more than 55% of co-occurring HOUST and RSAFS upticks over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates while capacity utilization remains elevated, indicating supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and force equity multiples to contract over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of INDPRO deceleration episodes, or the rolling Spearman correlation between INDPRO momentum and SPX returns exceeds 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly correlates with rising corporate earnings per share, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as fundamental valuation anchors shift higher over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the 20-day forward return median is negative or flat across all quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, raising real interest rates and compressing equity valuations, which manifests as negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if 5-day forward returns following a 0.3% CPI MoM print are positive in more than 50% of observed instances over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment validate a self-reinforcing labor market tightness that supports wage-driven consumption and corporate revenue growth, generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero directional accuracy in more than 60% of PAYEMS expansion and UNRATE contraction co-occurrences across 3 rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production stagnates while real GDP growth slows, confirming a synchronized demand and supply contraction that triggers earnings downgrades and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of cases where both INDPRO and GDPC1 show negative quarter-over-quarter momentum.
